---
title: "Run your first pipeline"
description: "From raw FASTQ files to an integrated, clustered object."
order: 0
---

Run a full single-cell pipeline on real, public data: raw 10x FASTQ files →
count matrices (Cell Ranger) → a filtered, normalized cohort → one batch-corrected,
clustered object. This is a **real run**, not a toy: you download a few GB of
FASTQs and references, and Cell Ranger needs a good chunk of memory and time.

This walkthrough uses **local Nextflow**. The exact same workflows run on Seqera
(`tw launch`) or through the Viash Hub web form, see the
[execution guides](../guides/index.qmd) for those routes.

## 1 · Set up Nextflow and Docker

Everything else ships inside containers, so this is all you install: a Java
runtime (Nextflow needs it), Nextflow, and Docker.

**1a · Java prerequisite.** Commands per OS. Java 17+ is recommended (11 is the
minimum).

::: {.panel-tabset}

### macOS

```bash
# install Homebrew (https://brew.sh) first, then:
brew install openjdk@17
```

### Ubuntu / Debian

```bash
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install openjdk-17-jdk unzip curl
```

### RHEL / Fedora

```bash
sudo yum install java-17-openjdk-devel unzip curl
```

### Arch

```bash
sudo pacman -Syy jdk17-openjdk unzip curl
```

### Windows

Use [WSL2](https://learn.microsoft.com/windows/wsl/install) with Ubuntu, then
follow the Ubuntu / Debian commands above.

:::

Check it with `java -version`.

**1b · Nextflow.**

```bash
curl -s https://get.nextflow.io | bash
sudo mv nextflow /usr/local/bin/
nextflow -version
```

**1c · Docker.** Install [Docker Desktop](https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/)
(macOS/Windows) or Docker Engine (Linux), start it, then check `docker --version`.

::: {.callout-warning}
**Pin your Nextflow version.** OpenPipeline's workflows don't yet run under the
strict language syntax enforced by the newest Nextflow releases. Pin a compatible
version before every run:

```bash
export NXF_VER=25.10.2
```
:::

Finally, tell Nextflow where to pull the pipeline from (one time):

```bash
mkdir -p "$HOME/.nextflow"
cat >> "$HOME/.nextflow/scm" <<'EOM'
providers.vsh.platform = "gitea"
providers.vsh.server = "packages.viash-hub.com"
EOM
```

## 2 · Get the data

The run uses two public 10x Genomics PBMC datasets (5' gene expression + VDJ +
antibody capture). Each 10x dataset page lists ready-made `curl` commands; the
loops below fetch both datasets' libraries and unpack them into a local `data/`
folder:

```bash
mkdir -p data && cd data

# Dataset 1 — Human PBMC, 10k cells, 5' v2
base1="https://cf.10xgenomics.com/samples/cell-vdj/5.0.0/sc5p_v2_hs_PBMC_10k"
for lib in 5gex t b 5fb; do
  curl -O "${base1}/sc5p_v2_hs_PBMC_10k_${lib}_fastqs.tar"
  tar -xf "sc5p_v2_hs_PBMC_10k_${lib}_fastqs.tar"
done

# Dataset 2 — Human PBMC, 10k cells, TotalSeq-C, 5' GEM-X
# (confirm the download URL on the dataset page)
base2="https://cf.10xgenomics.com/samples/cell-exp/9.0.0/10k_Human_PBMC_TotalSeqC_5p_gemx"
for lib in gex tcr ig ab; do
  curl -O "${base2}/10k_Human_PBMC_TotalSeqC_5p_gemx_${lib}_fastqs.tar"
  tar -xf "10k_Human_PBMC_TotalSeqC_5p_gemx_${lib}_fastqs.tar"
done

cd ..
```

The transcriptome, VDJ, and antibody **feature reference** (TotalSeq-C panel) are
all fetched automatically from URLs in the `param_list` below, so FASTQs are the
only thing you download by hand.

After both downloads, `data/` holds one folder per library:

```
data/
├── sc5p_v2_hs_PBMC_10k_5gex_fastqs/                # sample 1 · Gene Expression
├── sc5p_v2_hs_PBMC_10k_t_fastqs/                   # sample 1 · VDJ-T
├── sc5p_v2_hs_PBMC_10k_b_fastqs/                   # sample 1 · VDJ-B
├── sc5p_v2_hs_PBMC_10k_5fb_fastqs/                 # sample 1 · Antibody Capture
├── 10k_Human_PBMC_TotalSeqC_5p_gemx_gex_fastqs/    # sample 2 · Gene Expression
├── 10k_Human_PBMC_TotalSeqC_5p_gemx_tcr_fastqs/    # sample 2 · VDJ-T
├── 10k_Human_PBMC_TotalSeqC_5p_gemx_ig_fastqs/     # sample 2 · VDJ-B
└── 10k_Human_PBMC_TotalSeqC_5p_gemx_ab_fastqs/     # sample 2 · Antibody Capture
```

Each folder holds that library's `*_R1_*.fastq.gz` / `*_R2_*.fastq.gz` files.

## 3 · Ingest with Cell Ranger

`ingestion/cellranger_multi` aligns each library and writes one `.h5mu` per
sample; under the hood OpenPipeline 4.2.0 runs
[Cell Ranger `multi`](https://www.10xgenomics.com/support/software/cell-ranger/latest/analysis/running-pipelines/cr-5p-multi)
10. Describe the samples and their libraries in a `param_list`
(`params_cellranger.yaml`):

```yaml
# params_cellranger.yaml
param_list:
  - id: sc5p_v2_hs_PBMC_10k
    input:
      - data/sc5p_v2_hs_PBMC_10k_5gex_fastqs/*_R*.fastq.gz
      - data/sc5p_v2_hs_PBMC_10k_t_fastqs/*_R*.fastq.gz
      - data/sc5p_v2_hs_PBMC_10k_b_fastqs/*_R*.fastq.gz
      - data/sc5p_v2_hs_PBMC_10k_5fb_fastqs/*_R*.fastq.gz
    library_id: [PBMC_10k_5gex, PBMC_10k_t, PBMC_10k_b, PBMC_10k_5fb]
    library_type: [Gene Expression, VDJ-T, VDJ-B, Antibody Capture]
  - id: 10k_Human_PBMC_TotalSeqC_5p_gemx
    input:
      - data/10k_Human_PBMC_TotalSeqC_5p_gemx_gex_fastqs/*_R*.fastq.gz
      - data/10k_Human_PBMC_TotalSeqC_5p_gemx_tcr_fastqs/*_R*.fastq.gz
      - data/10k_Human_PBMC_TotalSeqC_5p_gemx_ig_fastqs/*_R*.fastq.gz
      - data/10k_Human_PBMC_TotalSeqC_5p_gemx_ab_fastqs/*_R*.fastq.gz
    library_id: [TotalSeqC_gex, TotalSeqC_tcr, TotalSeqC_ig, TotalSeqC_ab]
    library_type: [Gene Expression, VDJ-T, VDJ-B, Antibody Capture]
feature_reference: "<TotalSeq-C feature-reference CSV URL, from the dataset page>"
gex_reference: https://cf.10xgenomics.com/supp/cell-exp/refdata-gex-GRCh38-2020-A.tar.gz
vdj_reference: https://cf.10xgenomics.com/supp/cell-vdj/refdata-cellranger-vdj-GRCh38-alts-ensembl-7.0.0.tar.gz
publish_dir: results/ingestion
```

Each entry is one **sample**; the `input` / `library_id` / `library_type` lists
line up row-for-row to describe that sample's libraries. The
[Process many samples](../guides/process-many-samples.qmd) guide covers the full model.

```bash
nextflow run https://packages.viash-hub.com/vsh/openpipeline.git \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/ingestion/cellranger_multi/main.nf \
  -params-file params_cellranger.yaml
```

## 4 · Process the samples

`multiomics/process_samples` filters, normalizes, selects features, and runs PCA,
per sample then across the cohort, emitting one merged object. Point it at the
Cell Ranger outputs from step 3 (`params_process.yaml`):

```yaml
# params_process.yaml
param_list:
  - id: sc5p_v2_hs_PBMC_10k
    input: results/ingestion/sc5p_v2_hs_PBMC_10k.cellranger_multi.output_h5mu.h5mu
  - id: 10k_Human_PBMC_TotalSeqC_5p_gemx
    input: results/ingestion/10k_Human_PBMC_TotalSeqC_5p_gemx.cellranger_multi.output_h5mu.h5mu
rna_min_counts: 50           # thresholds you'd set from a QC report
rna_min_genes_per_cell: 50
publish_dir: results/processed
```

```bash
nextflow run https://packages.viash-hub.com/vsh/openpipeline.git \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/multiomics/process_samples/main.nf \
  -params-file params_process.yaml
```

## 5 · Integrate

`integration/scvi_leiden` batch-corrects across samples, then clusters and
computes a UMAP on the corrected embedding (`params_integrate.yaml`):

```yaml
# params_integrate.yaml
id: PBMC_scvi
input: results/processed/merged.process_samples.output.h5mu
obs_batch: sample_id
publish_dir: results/integrated
```

```bash
nextflow run https://packages.viash-hub.com/vsh/openpipeline.git \
  -r 4.2.0 -latest -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/workflows/integration/scvi_leiden/main.nf \
  -params-file params_integrate.yaml
```

To stack a second method (as the reference runs do, Harmony on top of scVI), see
[Integration](../guides/integrate.qmd).

## 6 · Read the output

The final `.h5mu` holds the whole result, per modality:

```
MuData
 └─ .mod
     └─ rna
         ├─ .layers["log_normalized"]        # normalized, log1p counts
         ├─ .obs["sample_id"]                # which sample each cell came from
         ├─ .obsm["X_pca"]                   # PCA from process_samples
         ├─ .obsm["X_scvi_integrated"]       # batch-corrected embedding (scVI)
         ├─ .obs["scvi_integration_leiden"]  # clusters on the integrated embedding
         └─ .obsm["X_scvi_umap"]             # UMAP for plotting
```
